incident_reconstruction.impact_direction: Frontal impact, likely from opposite direction
incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The vehicle was likely struck head-on by another vehicle that was on the wrong side of the road. The impact appears to have been at a relatively low speed, given the minor scratches reported. The damage is consistent with a frontal collision, but the exact extent of the damage is not provided.
reconstruction_bullets: The claimant reported being hit by a car on the wrong side, which suggests a head-on or frontal impact.
The vehicle's front end, particularly the bumper and possibly the bonnet, show signs of damage consistent with a frontal collision.
The lack of extensive damage suggests the impact speed was relatively low.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The Tata Altroz XZ Plus was driving on the correct side of the road when suddenly a vehicle on the wrong side collided with it.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle on the wrong side hit the Tata Altroz head-on, causing minor scratches and damage to the front end.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The driver assessed the damage and reported minor scratches, likely to the front bumper and possibly other frontal components.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence, although not extensively detailed, supports a frontal impact scenario, consistent with the claimant's story of being hit by a vehicle on the wrong side.

incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 70% | Story Match: Yes

decision_reasons: Fraud score of 40 exceeds the Approve threshold of less than 40, indicating a Medium fraud risk
Agent 1's damage assessment indicates no visible damage observed, inconsistent with the claim description
Multiple vehicles visible in claim images suggest a possible staged collision scenario (FS-002)
The garage estimate amount of ₹8000 may be inflated, considering the minor severity of the incident
reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment indicates minor severity but no visible damage observed
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence reveals a Medium fraud score of 40 and possible staged collision scenario (FS-002)
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction confirms damage matches the story with 70% confidence
Step 4: Context & Policy verification confirms location and no obvious coverage exclusions
Step 5: Historical Precedents suggest similar cases like HIST-012, but the fraud score and inconsistencies warrant escalation
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to escalation
Step 7: Final Decision is to Escalate the claim for further investigation

settlement_breakdown.assumptions: Parts/labour split assumed 70/30. Depreciation applied to the parts component per IRDAI age schedule. GST 18% on labour. Compulsory deductible Rs.1,000. Salvage assumed Rs.0 (no total loss).
